name: UI/UX playbook (hub)
description: Token-safe hub routine that routes to focused UI/UX modules (PM↔Design, metrics, visual principles, aesthetics prompts).

metadata.intent: Use the right UI/UX principles module for the current task without loading a giant doc

must_use: pi validate (use existing built-in checks before inventing new rules)
must_not: Bulk-load every .pi/routines file into an agent context
conventions: Keep only the hub + a few referenced leaves in active agent context (cap is enforced by Pi CLI).

steps.description: If you’re in sprint kickoff and design is pushing back on a feature spec, open `ui-ux-for-product-managers` + `ui-ux-design-process-5-stages` and rewrite the spec as: user→problem→why→constraints→success metrics.
If stakeholders need evidence, open `ui-ux-metrics-for-pms` and pick 2–3 measurable outcomes (task success rate, time on task, CSAT/NPS) tied to the roadmap decision.
If the team needs interface-level guardrails, open `ui-ux-ui-principles-7` (checklist) + `ui-ux-visual-design-principles-12` (composition) and apply them directly to the current flow.

If you need distinctive, production-grade UI (avoid 'AI slop'), open `frontend-design-distinctive-ui-skill` and use it as an agent prompt prefix (typography, color tokens, motion, backgrounds, constraints).

If you’re using shadcn/ui and want its principles, scaling structure, blocks, and ecosystem libraries (Radix/CVA/RHF/Zod/Sonner/etc.), open `shadcn-ui-playbook` and then install only the relevant leaf modules.

If you’re using Chakra UI (style props, theming, dark mode) and want comparisons vs Tailwind/MUI/Ant/Theme UI plus runtime tradeoffs, open `chakra-ui-playbook` and install only the leaf modules you need.
If you’re choosing a React UI library for 2025 (MUI/Chakra/Radix/shadcn/Ant/Mantine/etc.), open `react-ui-libraries-2025` and pick based on ownership + a11y + customization + performance constraints.
If you need deep MUI customization without exploding the API, open `mui-customization-slot-strategy` and apply: slots for replaceable parts + slotProps/componentsProps for scoped behavior tweaks.

If you’re refactoring React components with too many boolean props, render props, or prop drilling, open `react-composition-playbook` and apply composition patterns (compound components + provider-owned state + explicit variants).

If the task is React Native or Expo (lists, Reanimated, gestures, navigation, images, native menus/modals, monorepo autolinking), open `react-native-skills-playbook` and load only the leaf modules you need.

If the task is React View Transitions (`<ViewTransition>`, `addTransitionType`, shared-element morphs, Suspense reveals, Next.js `experimental.viewTransition` / `Link` `transitionTypes`), open `react-view-transitions-playbook` and import only the referenced leaf modules (start with `react-view-transitions-css-recipes` + `react-view-transitions-implementation-workflow`).

Before adding new heuristics, run `pi validate` and use the existing checks: semantic: `ux-copy-tone`, `animation-ux`, `navigation-ia`; deterministic: `a11y-images`, `no-hardcoded-hex`, `no-z-index-chaos`, `no-magic-dimensions`, `no-inline-style-object`.

To keep routing token-safe, install only the leaf modules you need (each becomes a routine file under `.pi/routines/`): `pi routine import ui-ux-for-product-managers`, `pi routine import ui-ux-metrics-for-pms`, `pi routine import ui-ux-ui-principles-7`, `pi routine import ui-ux-visual-design-principles-12`, `pi routine import frontend-design-distinctive-ui-skill`.
